# Header Relay

> Header Relay is a Chrome extension for API development and testing. It captures selected response headers and relays fixed or captured values to later browser requests for developer-configured target origins.

## Official links

- Website: https://hsblabs.github.io/header-relay/
- Chrome Web Store: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/olmfkdclaloaacojbgaabokehlbphilf
- Privacy policy: https://hsblabs.github.io/header-relay/privacy
- Cookie feature announcement: https://hsblabs.github.io/header-relay/cookie
- Cookie FAQ: https://hsblabs.github.io/header-relay/cookie/faq
- Changelog: https://hsblabs.github.io/header-relay/changelogs

## Key facts

- Intended for local, staging, internal, and other controlled API testing environments.
- Header capture and attachment apply only to enabled profiles and configured target origins.
- Configured excluded paths remain untouched.
- Profiles can send Fixed Cookies and track registered Cookie names from Set-Cookie responses.
- Header Relay does not read or modify Chrome's cookie store and does not request the cookies permission.
- Tracked Cookie values remain in the current browser session and are never written to audit logs or analytics.
- Profiles, captured values, session state, and audit logs are not sent to the developer or an external data service.
- Audit logs retain at most 1,000 recent entries for no longer than 7 days.
- Logged URLs omit query strings and fragments.

## Languages

- English: https://hsblabs.github.io/header-relay/
- Japanese: https://hsblabs.github.io/header-relay/ja
- Korean: https://hsblabs.github.io/header-relay/ko
